name: Community State Bank
description_short: Community State Bank Paton, IA — Community State Bank is a locally owned independent bank founded in 1956 serving Paton, Iowa and surrounding counties w...
description_long:
Community State Bank was established in 1956 by a group of local businessmen and has remained a locally owned and independent institution for over 65 years. The bank is headquartered in Paton, Iowa, a small community of approximately 255 people located in the northeast corner of Greene County, 70 miles northwest of Des Moines. The bank serves not only Paton but also neighboring communities across Greene, Boone, Webster, and Hamilton counties.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of traditional banking products including multiple checking account types (Regular Checking and NOW Checking), savings accounts (Passbook Savings and Money Market Savings), Certificates of Deposit, and Individual Retirement Accounts (Traditional IRAs, Roth IRAs, and SEPs). Community State Bank also provides various loan products with competitive terms customized to meet customer needs. In addition to in-person banking, the institution offers online and mobile banking platforms that provide 24/7 access to accounts and banking services.

What distinguishes Community State Bank is its emphasis on local ownership and personalized service. As an independent bank rather than part of a larger chain, the institution positions itself as deeply embedded in its community with a focus on providing "a very personable and professional atmosphere" to clients. The bank's longevity since 1956 and continued independence reflects stability and local commitment.

For consumers in rural Iowa seeking traditional banking services, Community State Bank offers standard products at a small-town institution. However, as a small regional bank, it may have limited technological features compared to larger banks or online-only institutions, and service availability is restricted to its specific geographic footprint in north-central Iowa. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: In-person banking at 207 South Main Street, Paton, Iowa
pros: Locally owned and independent bank with over 65 years of operating history since 1956
Offers both online and mobile banking for 24/7 access to accounts and services
Multiple account types including Regular Checking, NOW Checking, Passbook Savings, and Money Market Savings
Provides retirement account options including Traditional IRAs, Roth IRAs, and SEP IRAs
Offers Certificates of Deposit for fixed-term savings with competitive rates
Customizable loan products designed to meet individual customer needs
Personalized and professional service atmosphere emphasizing community relationships
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Paton, Iowa and surrounding counties in north-central Iowa
Small institution may lack advanced technological features available at larger national banks
No mention of specific loan types, rates, terms, or APR information on website
Limited branch presence compared to regional or national banking alternatives
No information provided about deposit insurance coverage or FDIC membership on reviewed pages
best_for: Residents of Paton, Iowa and surrounding Greene, Boone, Webster, and Hamilton counties seeking local banking relationships
Small business owners and farmers in rural north-central Iowa needing personalized banking services
Customers prioritizing community-focused banking over large national bank chains
Savers seeking traditional products like CDs and IRAs from a stable, independent institution
diagnosis: Community State Bank is best for rural Iowans in its service territory who value local, personalized banking relationships and traditional financial products. The main caveat is that its small size and geographic limitation to north-central Iowa make it unsuitable for customers outside this region or those seeking advanced digital banking features typical of larger institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
